name: Buttermilk Fried Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, salt, and pepper until well combined.
2. Cut the chicken into pieces if desired.
3. Place the chicken in a shallow container and pour the buttermilk mixture over top. Cover and let stand for 20 minutes, turning the pieces once to ensure they are coated evenly.
4. Remove the chicken from the buttermilk mixture and discard the liquid.
5. Shake each piece of chicken well in a separate bowl filled with flour, making sure it is fully coated on all sides.
6. Heat the vegetable oil in a deep fryer or heavy-based pot to 350°F (175°C).
7. Carefully lower the floured chicken pieces into the hot oil using tongs and cook until golden brown, turning occasionally to ensure even cooking on both sides. This should take about 10-12 minutes.
8. Lower the temperature of the oil to 275°F (135°C) and continue frying for another 25 minutes or until the internal temperature of the chicken reaches 165°F (74°C). Cover the pot to help retain heat and speed up cooking time.
9. Remove the chicken from the oil using a slotted spoon and place it on a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
10. Uncover the pot, increase the heat back to 350°F (175°C), and fry for an additional 5 minutes until the chicken is crispy and cooked through.
